search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk CG95 atypical chest discomfort history associated symptoms ECG
site:dha.gov.ae chest pain telehealth guideline Dubai
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic emergency, troponin, imaging, or treatment instructions
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Atypical wording does not exclude a cardiac or other serious cause.
Urgency, tests, diagnosis, treatment, and referral remain clinician decisions.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with recent chest discomfort undergoing clinician assessment; the workflow does not establish cause or acuity.
setting_applicability: Initial, urgent, primary, or outpatient assessment; current severe symptoms require direct emergency assessment outside this template.
UAE_applicability: International NICE evidence requires UAE emergency, diagnostic, referral, and facility-pathway adaptation; the existing DHA chest-pain source remains separately available.
recency_verification: NICE CG95 last updated 2016-11-30 was reviewed on 2026-07-13; major recency gap remains.
superseded_check: CG95 remains current NICE guidance but is old; newer acute coronary syndrome guidance was reviewed separately and not used to infer diagnosis.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The NICE section supports documenting chest-discomfort timing, characteristics, associated symptoms, cardiovascular history and risk, examination, and already reviewed ECG or tests without inferring an acute coronary syndrome.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting pain timing and characteristics, associated symptoms, cardiovascular history and risk, vital signs and examination, and clinician-reviewed ECG or test context; urgent conclusions remain clinician-led.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The information section supports recording uncertainty, patient questions, reviewed-test explanation, clinician-provided safety-netting, and follow-up without generating advice.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.

unresolved_source_gaps: Current symptom status, exact location and character, radiation, triggers, duration, associated symptoms, risk factors, prior disease and tests, vital signs, examination, ECG, biomarkers, diagnosis, urgency, treatment, referral, and safety-net content remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
